name: memoryai-vsx
description: One brain. Every AI you use. Forever. Shared long-term memory for Claude, Cursor, AI assistant, and any IDE that follows.

memoryai.endpoint.description: MemoryAI API endpoint. Use http://localhost:8420 for self-hosted.

memoryai.contextWindow.description: Your model's context window, in tokens. Pick 200000 for most models (Claude, GPT, Sonnet) or 1000000 for 1M-window models (Opus 1M, Gemini). You can also type any exact number. This drives the default Critical % below (≤200K → 95%, >200K → 30%).
enumDescriptions: 200K — Claude, GPT-4o, Sonnet, Haiku, most models.

memoryai.criticalPercent.description: Critical threshold as a percent of the context window — the fill level at which the guard forces a save + shows the 'context is full' notice. Leave at 0 to auto-follow the window (≤200K → 95, >200K → 30). Set any value 5–99 to override — e.g. raise a 1M model from 30 to 35 or 40 to keep more context before saving. The soft 'compact' point where silent saving begins is derived automatically at 85% of the effective value.

memoryai.privateMode.description: Local-only mode for NDA / regulated data. Reserved for a future release.

memoryai.autoSpawn.description: Kiro only. When the live conversation reaches the Critical fill level, automatically open a fresh chat at the next idle turn boundary (after the agent finishes answering). Everything is saved first, so the new chat restores full context via recall + bootstrap and continues where you left off. Turn off to keep the manual 'open a new chat' notice instead.

memoryai.statusBar.description: Status-bar display format. full = brain size + monthly activity + savings (recommended). savings = $ saved today only. tokens = brain volume only. compact = monthly activity only. off = hide.
enumDescriptions: Brain volume — total memories and storage.
This month's activity — stores and recalls.
